task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function does not handle edge case when input list contains duplicates.
trace: I approached this task by first sorting the input list `nums` in ascending order, ensuring that the largest and second-largest values are at the end of the list. I then checked if there were fewer than two distinct values in the list to handle the edge case where there is only one unique number or no distinct numbers at all. If there are at least two distinct values, I returned the second-last element from the sorted list which represents the second largest value.
